definitions.definition: The animal kingdom is a classification of living organisms that are multicellular, eukaryotic, and heterotrophic. They are capable of movement and possess a complex body structure.
The kingdom Animalia is a taxonomic classification of multicellular, eukaryotic, and heterotrophic organisms that are capable of movement and possess a complex body structure.
A subkingdom of animals that have a solid mouth and a complete body cavity. In protostomia, the mouth develops first, and the anus develops later.
A subkingdom of animals that have a hollow mouth and an incomplete body cavity. In deuterostomia, the anus develops first, and the mouth develops later.
A taxonomic rank that includes a group of animals with similar body structure and embryonic development.
A taxonomic rank that includes a group of animals with similar body structure, embryonic development, and other characteristics.
The study of the shape and structure of living organisms.
The study of the development of an embryo from fertilization to birth.
Animals that do not have a body cavity. They have a solid body wall.
Animals that have a body cavity that is not a true coelom. They have a fluid-filled cavity.
Animals that have a true body cavity. They have a fluid-filled cavity.
The body of an animal can be divided into two symmetrical halves by a single plane.
No definite symmetry in the body of an animal.
A phylum of animals that have stinging cells called cnidoblasts.
A phylum of animals that have a unique water vascular system.
A phylum of animals that have a notochord, a dorsal hollow nerve cord, and pharyngeal slits.
A subphylum of chordates that have a vertebral column.
A subphylum of chordates that do not have a vertebral column.
A type of embryonic development in which the cells divide in a spiral pattern.
A stage of embryonic development in which the cells of the blastula undergo a series of movements to form the gastrula.
